title: Irish Brie and Xtra Gratin
description: A rich and creamy gratin featuring Irish cheddar and brie cheese, perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the springy onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, combine the Irish cheddar, brie cheese, and xtra cheese. Stir until melted and smooth.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of springy onions. Top with a layer of grated cheese.
Repeat the layering process, starting with the onions and ending with a layer of cheese.
Roll out the gratin potatoes to a thickness of about 1/8 inch. Cut into long, thin strips and place on top of the cheese.
Dot the top of the potatoes with unsalted butter.
Sprinkle the all-purpose flour over the potatoes.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown and the cheese is bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
